phrases.text_local: Kde jsou fresky s hornickou tematikou?
phrases.cultural_note: Czechs often use landmarks like 'side chapels' rather than compass directions. 'Po levé straně' (on the left side) is the most natural way to give indoor directions.

likely_response.local: V bočních kaplích po levé straně.
likely_response.translation_en: In the side chapels on the left.

responses.english: In the side chapels on the left.
responses.local: V bočních kaplích po levé straně.

phrases.intent_en: Where do I get the audio guide
phrases.text_local: Kde si můžu půjčit audio průvodce?
phrases.cultural_note: In Czech churches and attractions, the pokladna (ticket desk) almost always handles audio guide rentals. 'Hned' (right/immediately) is commonly used for precise proximity.

likely_response.translation_en: At the ticket desk, right by the entrance.

responses.english: At the ticket desk, right by the entrance.

phrases.intent_en: Is photography permitted in the Gothic nave
phrases.cultural_note: Many Czech historic interiors allow photography but strictly prohibit flash to protect artwork. 'Jen bez blesku' is the standard, terse response from staff.
